author: Ana R.
comment: The riverside park needs proper lighting after sunset — many of us stop running there in winter because it simply feels unsafe.

author: Ben K.
comment: Please keep the wild meadow section! It's the only place in the city where my kids have seen fireflies.

author: Carmen L.
comment: Me parece fundamental que el nuevo diseño incluya sombra natural. En verano el parque es un horno y los árboles tardan décadas en crecer.

author: Diego M.
comment: A dedicated bike lane through the park would stop the constant conflicts between cyclists and people walking dogs.

author: Elena V.
comment: The playground equipment is fine, what's missing are benches and toilets near it. Grandparents do half the childcare here.

author: Farid H.
comment: I run a small café next to the east gate. Construction timing matters enormously to us — please phase the works and keep at least one entrance open.

author: Grace O.
comment: Astroturf instead of grass would be a disaster for drainage and for the temperature of the whole area in summer.

author: Hugo P.
comment: cool

author: Iris T.
comment: The skate spot brings teenagers to the park — don't remove it, improve it. Give them lights and water fountains like everyone else.

author: Jonas W.
comment: Every consultation promises accessibility and every park still has gravel paths my wheelchair can't handle. Smooth, firm surfaces on ALL main routes please.

author: Karla S.
comment: Community garden plots would turn passive green space into something people actually take care of. Waiting lists elsewhere in the city are two years long.

author: Liam N.
comment: I'd rather have fewer fancy features and more maintenance budget. The last renovation looked great for exactly one year.

author: Marta G.
comment: Dogs need a fenced off-leash area. Right now every conflict in the park is about dogs because there is nowhere legal to let them run.

author: Noor A.
comment: Please plan the sports courts away from the residential edge. The current basketball court is wonderful for players and miserable for the people living five meters from it.

author: Oscar D.
comment: An amphitheater sounds nice but we already have three underused stages within two kilometers. Spend the money on trees and water.

author: Paula F.
comment: The pond should be restored as habitat, not turned into a splash pad. Herons still visit it every spring.

author: Quentin B.
comment: As a night-shift worker I use the park at odd hours. 24h access matters — please don't fence and lock it like the north park.

author: Rosa C.
comment: Free drinking water fountains, that's it, that's my comment. It's 2026 and we still have none.

author: Sam E.
comment: If you add food kiosks, please reserve at least one spot for local vendors instead of the same chain that's in every other park.

author: Tessa J.
comment: The park's history as an old railway yard could be told with a small info trail — kids love the old signal post and nobody knows what it is.
